personalInfo.name: Fabio Aurelio D'Asaro
personalInfo.title: Postdoctoral Researcher in Logic (PHIL-02/A)
personalInfo.bio: I am a researcher in Logic (PHIL-02/A) working at the interface of formal epistemology and AI, probabilistic and temporal reasoning, argumentation, and logic programming (ASP/ILASP) with teaching and supervision spanning Logic & Philosophy of Science, AI, Theoretical, Applied Computer Science, Epistemology of Big Data and Logic Programming.

education.supervisor: Rob Miller, Antonis Bikakis and Luke Dickens
education.description: My PhD thesis explored how to model uncertainty (Probability Theory, in particular) and knowledge-producing actions in a popular narrative-based framework for Reasoning About Actions called the Event Calculus. Excerpts of my PhD thesis were published in top Logic and AI journals and conferences, including Artificial Intelligence (AIJ) and the International Conference on Logic Programming and Nonmonotonic Reasoning (LPNMR).

education.degree: MSc in Pure Mathematics and Mathematical Logic
education.thesis: Analogical Reasoning in Unary Inductive Logic
education.description: Final grade: Distinction (equivalent to 110/110 cum laude in the Italian grading system).

education.thesis: La Tesi di Church-Turing e il suo rapporto con alcuni nuovi modelli di calcolo
education.description: Dissertation: "La Tesi di Church-Turing e il suo rapporto con alcuni nuovi modelli di calcolo" (en: "The Church-Turing Thesis and its relation with some new computation models")

experience.description: I am currently a postdoctoral researcher at the University of Salento in the Department of Human Studies (SSD PHIL-02/A). I am working in the context of Project FAIR–Future AI Research (PE00000013) under the NRRP MUR program funded by NextGenerationEU, Spoke 6 ("Symbiotic AI"), PI: Paolo Baldi.
responsibilities: Research in Logic for ethical human–AI collaboration
eDefAI (Ethical Design for AI)
Symbiotic AI development

experience.description: Teaching across Philosophy, Information Studies, and Computer/Data Science programs. Delivering 100+ lecture hours per year on average with consistently strong student evaluations.
responsibilities: Logic and Philosophy of Science (BA Philosophy)
Epistemology of Big Data (MSc Data Analytics)
Computational Epistemology (MSc Artificial Intelligence)
Logic and Philosophy of Science in Physiotherapy (BSc Physiotherapy)
Laboratory of Computer Science and Multimedia Technologies (BA Philosophy)
Laboratory of Artificial Intelligence and Neuroscience (PhD Human Sciences)

experience.description: Assistant Professor researching Uncertain and Epistemic Reasoning, Temporal and Probabilistic Logics, Logic Programming, Reasoning about Actions and Change, Bounded Reasoning, Formal Argumentation, and their applications to AI and Explainable AI.
responsibilities: Research in Logic and AI
Teaching undergraduate and graduate courses
Company secondment at REVO SPAC (12 months)

experience.description: During my period as a post-doctoral fellow in the Logic Group at the University of Milan, I worked on uncertain, depth-bounded and epistemic logics.

I worked on the AVATEA Project, which aims to develop an AI system to support the rehabilitation process of children with neuro-motor disorders. Furthermore, I carried out research on explainable AI systems for robotics and preference learning.
Worked as a Lecturer and Lab Demonstrator for many undergraduate and postgraduate courses offered by the UCL Department of Information Studies.
responsibilities: INST0074 Machine Reasoning for Artificial Intelligence
INST0060 Foundations of Machine Learning and Data Science
INST0019 Introduction to Programming and Scripting
INST0029 Server Programming and Structured Data
INST0004 Programming 2

experience.description: Worked under the supervision of prof. Settimo Termini and Marco Elio Tabacchi as a fully funded graduate research assistant in the NEVERLOST Project (PO-FESR 2007-2013). Developed and implemented algorithms for optimal antenna displacement using soft-computing techniques.
responsibilities: Algorithm development using Fuzzy Logic and Genetic Algorithms
Serious game development
Publications on Fuzzy Logic foundations

experience.description: During my internship at ICAR CNR I collaborated on the IMPULSO project, developing a path visualization tool using Google Maps APIs.

description.en: Developed Handwritten recognition tools, entity matching, and eligibility pre-screening modules; requirements analysis with REVO Insurance; supervision of collaborators hired on the project. Company secondment at REVO SPAC (12 months).
description.it: Sviluppo di strumenti di riconoscimento della scrittura a mano, entity matching e moduli di pre-screening; analisi dei requisiti con REVO Insurance; supervisione di collaboratori assunti sul progetto. Distacco aziendale presso REVO SPAC (12 mesi).

description.en: Proponent and internal collaborator/PRIN participant (University of Milan); external collaborator after institutional move (EThOS Group, University of Verona). Mainly worked on formal frameworks for trust/bias and tooling.
description.it: Proponente e collaboratore interno/partecipante PRIN (Università di Milano); collaboratore esterno dopo trasferimento istituzionale (EThOS Group, Università di Verona). Ha lavorato principalmente su framework formali per trust/bias e tooling.

description.en: Designed and implemented a runtime epistemic probabilistic Event Calculus architecture for sensor fusion and decision-making in rehabilitation serious games. Worked on an AI system to support the rehabilitation process of children with neuro-motor disorders.
description.it: Progettato e implementato un'architettura runtime di Event Calculus probabilistico epistemico per fusione di sensori e decision-making in serious games di riabilitazione. Ha lavorato su un sistema di IA per supportare il processo di riabilitazione di bambini con disturbi neuro-motori.
